summary:
The most frequent location for a Melanotic Neuroectodermal Tumor of Infancy appears to be within facial structures such as the maxilla based on its close association with areas prone to swelling and presence near mucous membranes; this conclusion draws from general patterns observed in related infantile conditions affecting these regions. Thus, \(\boxed{\text{Maxilla}}\) seems highly probable amongst listed choices. To refine further:

The primary focus should remain clear about identifying critical aspects—namely understanding what constitutes an 'infant' tumor type specifically located at certain sites—and recognizing how specific body parts are more frequently involved compared to others when dealing with benign/malignant neoplasms presenting uniquely across different age groups including neonates through toddlers. Hence focusing strictly upon core elements leads us towards concluding that

\[ 
\boxed{\text{Maxilla}}
\] represents best fit according standard medical literature trends discussed here today concerning MNTI prevalence locations primarily involving head/face area especially around nasal/oral/pharyngeal interfaces which often exhibit higher susceptibility toward developing various types of congenital anomalies inclusive but not limited to those classified under category designated by term ‘Melanotic’ noted above contextually speaking .
